destination.vibe: A mid-sized Southern city with a world-class free art museum, one of the longest intact stretches of Victorian row houses in the US, a brewery-and-restaurant scene anchored in Scott's Addition and the Fan, and a James River cutting straight through downtown with swimming holes and herons accessible entirely on foot.

flights.outbound_dates: Late July 2026 (e.g. Jul 29) — mid-week departures cheapest
flights.notes: Spirit operates nonstop DTW→RIC. Ultra-low base fare but add bags and seat selection. Cheapest days: Tuesday/Wednesday departures.

flights.outbound_dates: Late July 2026 (flexible Jul 28–Aug 2 window) — midweek lowest
flights.notes: Delta runs frequent nonstops DTW→RIC. Roundtrip from $350–$450 in peak summer. Includes carry-on bag.

flights.outbound_dates: Flexible late July / early August 2026 — Tuesday/Wednesday lowest
flights.notes: American serves DTW→RIC via connection at Charlotte (CLT) or Philadelphia (PHL). Roundtrip from ~$280–$350.

lodging.name: Fan District Victorian Row House (Entire Home — Airbnb)
lodging.highlights: Entire Victorian row house on or near Strawberry Street — steps from restaurants, the VMFA, and Carytown, with the neighborhood feel that no hotel can replicate.

lodging.name: Graduate by Hilton Richmond
lodging.neighborhood: Downtown / Fan District edge (301 W Franklin St, near VCU)
lodging.highlights: University-district boutique hotel with rooftop terrace overlooking the VCU Institute for Contemporary Art — walkable to VMFA, the Fan, and downtown.

lodging.name: Quirk Hotel Richmond
lodging.highlights: Boutique gallery-hotel with rotating art installations, rooftop bar, and on-site restaurant Maple & Pine — Destination by Hyatt property in the heart of the arts district.

lodging.name: The Jefferson Hotel
lodging.neighborhood: Downtown Richmond (Franklin St at Adams St)
lodging.highlights: National Historic Landmark opened in 1895 — the staircase reportedly inspired Gone With the Wind, Lemaire restaurant is Richmond's finest dining.

itinerary.overview: Richmond is a mid-sized Southern city that punches well above its weight: a world-class free art museum (VMFA), one of the longest intact stretches of Victorian architecture in the US, a brewery-and-restaurant scene anchored in Scott's Addition and the Fan, and a James River cutting straight through downtown with swimming holes and Class IV rapids accessible entirely on foot. Ten days lets you sink into neighborhoods rather than race through them.

days.title: Arrival + The Fan Orientation
days.morning: Fly in from DTW, land at RIC. Rideshare to the Fan (~20 minutes). Walk the length of Monument Avenue from Belvidere Street west: the grand mansions, the empty pedestals where Confederate statues stood (only Arthur Ashe's statue remains), and the remarkable tree canopy.
days.afternoon: Garnett's Cafe (2001 Park Ave) for lunch: the Scuffletown Chicken Salad Sandwich. Then VMFA (200 N Arthur Ashe Blvd) — free admission. Kehinde Wiley's 'Rumors of War' out front, the Faberge collection (largest outside Russia), and the Art Nouveau galleries.
days.evening: Dinner at Beaucoup (111 N Robinson St) — Richmond's best seafood and oyster spot. Then The Jasper (3101 W Cary St) in Carytown — one of the best cocktail bars on the East Coast.

days.title: VMFA Deep Dive + Carytown
days.morning: Return to the VMFA at 10am. American galleries, African art collection, modern and contemporary floors.
days.afternoon: Carytown (W Cary St between Thompson and Boulevard): For the Love of Chocolate, World of Mirth, Mom's Siam (Thai, 3622 W Cary St — green curry is legendary), Gelati Celesti ice cream.
days.evening: Byrd Theatre (2908 W Cary St) — 1928 movie palace, films nightly $4–8, Mighty Wurlitzer organ on Fri/Sat. Or Can Can Brasserie (3120 W Cary St) for dinner.

days.title: Jackson Ward History + Soul Food
days.morning: Black History Museum & Cultural Center of Virginia (122 W Leigh St). Maggie L. Walker National Historic Site (600 N 2nd St) — free NPS site, ranger-led tours.
days.afternoon: Mama J's (415 N 1st St) — fried chicken, collard greens, mac and cheese. Walk the Jackson Ward mural corridors. Bill 'Bojangles' Robinson statue at Adams and Leigh.
days.evening: Restaurant Adarra (1 W Marshall St) — Basque pintxos with Virginia ingredients. Soul Taco (2005 W Broad St) for late-night tacos: root beer braised oxtail.

days.title: Church Hill + The James River
days.morning: Sub Rosa Bakery (620 N 25th St) — labor-intensive sourdough using Virginia grain. St. John's Church (2401 E Broad St) where Patrick Henry gave his 'Give Me Liberty' speech.
days.afternoon: Belle Isle via pedestrian suspension bridge — 54-acre island in the James, flat rocks, Civil War ruins, wade in the rocky pools. Then Tredegar Street along the north bank past the American Civil War Museum.
days.evening: Sam Miller's (1210 E Cary St) in Shockoe Slip — fresh seafood and raw bar.

days.title: Scott's Addition Brewery District
days.morning: Blanchard's Coffee. Then Brambly Park (2901 W Leigh St) — outdoor urban winery with lawn seating.
days.afternoon: Brewery crawl: The Veil Brewing Co. (2545 W Leigh St), Ardent Craft Ales (3200 W Leigh St), Strangeways Brewing (2277 Dabney Rd). ZZQ Texas Craft Barbeque (3201 W Moore St, Wed–Sun until sold out). Blue Bee Cider (1320 Dell Ave).
days.evening: Dinner at Lost Letter — beef tartare and butterkin squash gnocchi.

days.title: Maymont Estate + Oregon Hill + L'Opossum
days.morning: Maymont (1700 Hampton St, free admission) — Japanese Garden, Italian Garden, Arboretum, Virginia Wildlife Trail with black bears and bobcats.
days.afternoon: Oregon Hill overlook at South Pine Street for the best elevated river views. Pony Pasture Rapids (7200 Riverside Dr) — Richmond's most popular James River swimming spot.
days.evening: L'Opossum (626 China St) — French technique applied to eclectic Virginia-Southern mashups. Reservations essential. James Beard Award-recognized.

days.title: ICA VCU + Shockoe Bottom History
days.morning: ICA at VCU (601 W Broad St) — always free, Steven Holl-designed building.
days.afternoon: Virginia Holocaust Museum (2000 E Cary St, $10). 17th Street Farmers Market (100 N 17th St). Reconciliation Triangle acknowledging Richmond's role as the largest domestic slave trading hub pre-Civil War.
days.evening: Poe Museum 'Unhappy Hour' cocktail events (check poemuseum.org). Dinner at Addis Ethiopian (2306 Jefferson Ave).

days.title: Day Trip: Route 5 Historic Byway + Upper Shirley Vineyards
days.morning: Drive ~30 minutes east on Route 5 (historic scenic byway) to Upper Shirley Vineyards (600 Shirley Plantation Rd) — tastings from 11am. Pass Berkeley Plantation (birthplace of William Henry Harrison).
days.afternoon: Shirley Plantation (501 Shirley Plantation Rd) — oldest active plantation in the US (1613). Return via River Road. Late afternoon at Hardywood Park Craft Brewery (2410 Ownby Ln).
days.evening: Edo's Squid (411 N Harrison St) — loud Italian, garlic-forward pasta, best calamari in Richmond. Arrive by 5:30pm. Check The National (708 E Broad St) for music.

days.title: Fan Neighborhood Walk + Museum District + Celladora
days.morning: Slow Fan morning: walk Park Ave to Floyd Ave, Belvidere to Boulevard. Susie's Delicatessen for a bagel. Virginia Museum of History and Culture (428 N Arthur Ashe Blvd).
days.afternoon: Valentine Museum (1015 E Clay St) — city history, walking tours. North End Juice Co. for coffee. Another 90 min at VMFA cafe.
days.evening: Celladora (Fan District) — described by The Infatuation as the best restaurant in Richmond, natural wine focus, French-leaning small plates. Reservations essential.

days.title: Last Morning on the James + Farewell
days.morning: Belle Isle at sunrise or Pony Pasture for a last swim. Sub Rosa Bakery or Shyndigz (banana pudding cake). Monument Avenue one last time.
days.afternoon: Final lunch at Joe's Inn (205 N Shields Ave) — 73-year Richmond institution. Rideshare to RIC (~20–25 min from the Fan).

tips: The Fan is the best base — most restaurants, coffee shops, and bars within a 15-minute walk. Airbnb Victorian row houses give a neighborhood feel no hotel can match.
Pony Pasture (south bank, car/rideshare required) is safer and more popular for James River swimming than Belle Isle — the rocky pools are genuinely beautiful.
Restaurant scene is best Tue–Thu: L'Opossum, Celladora, and Beaucoup all require advance reservations; book before leaving Detroit.
VMFA is completely free for the permanent collection — 90 minutes twice beats one exhausting four-hour visit. The cafe is also excellent for lunch.
Scott's Addition: Uber there and walk between breweries. The Veil has lines on weekend afternoons — arrive at opening (noon Saturday).
ZZQ sells out by early afternoon (open Wed–Sun, 11am). Get there by 11:30am or miss the brisket — they're genuinely sold out by 2pm.

sources.title: The Infatuation: Best Restaurants in Richmond VA

destination.vibe: 90 miles north of Penn Station by Amtrak, Rhinebeck sits at the center of the best road cycling in the Northeast — 300-year-old country lanes through orchards and river estates, the Hudson at the bottom of every hill, the Catskills on the horizon, and the Shawangunk Ridge one bridge-crossing away.

flights.outbound_dates: July–August flexible (Tue/Wed cheapest; 4+ daily nonstops DTW→JFK)
flights.notes: DTW→JFK nonstop. Best bike policy of any major carrier: bikes as oversized checked bag ~$35/way. Average $179–$217 RT in July.

flights.outbound_dates: Early August (Tue Aug 4 cheapest; ~daily nonstops DTW→EWR)
flights.notes: DTW→EWR (Newark). Cheapest ticket but United's bike fee is ~$150 each way. EWR to Penn Station: Newark AirTrain + NJ Transit (~40min, ~$18).
DTW→JFK nonstop. $100 bike handling fee + 80-linear-inch / 50 lb hard case limit. Best backup if Delta is sold out.

transportation.name: Amtrak Empire Service — Penn Station NYC → Rhinecliff
transportation.description: Penn Station NYC to Rhinecliff station · scenic Hudson River route
transportation.notes: Runs 3–4x daily. Reserve bike space at booking — July fills fast.

lodging.name: Beekman Arms & Delamater Inn
lodging.highlights: America's oldest continuously operating inn (est. 1766) — roll out the front door onto back roads, free breakfast, free parking.

lodging.name: The Wick
lodging.highlights: Converted 1860s candle factory on Hudson's gallery-lined Warren Street — complimentary breakfast, Lil' Deb's Oasis around the corner.

lodging.name: Mirbeau Inn & Spa
lodging.highlights: Complimentary loaner bikes, full spa, pools, on-site dining — one mile from every back road in Dutchess County.

itinerary.overview: The Hudson Valley has been the best road cycling in the Northeast for decades. The roads around Rhinebeck, Red Hook, and Tivoli are genuinely quiet. You fly into JFK, take Amtrak 90 miles north to Rhinecliff, roll off the train, and you're immediately in back-road territory. Across the Hudson, the Shawangunk Ridge offers 15% pitches accessed via the Walkway Over the Hudson in Poughkeepsie.

days.title: Arrive — DTW → JFK → Rhinecliff
days.morning: Fly DTW→JFK (Delta, 1h52m nonstop). AirTrain + subway to Penn Station. Amtrak Empire Service to Rhinecliff ($10 bike reservation, July fills fast).
days.afternoon: Rhinecliff station is a 1873 Victorian depot. Mile from Rhinecliff up to Rhinebeck village. Bread Alone Bakery (45 E Market St) for pastry.
days.evening: Dinner at Terrapin (farm-to-table since 1998) or Aba's Falafel.

days.title: Rhinebeck → Bard → Tivoli → Red Hook — 34mi / 55km
days.morning: North on Route 9G, back roads through Bard College campus. Tivoli General (52 Broadway) for bialys.
days.afternoon: GioBatta Alimentari (48 Broadway, Tivoli) for lunch. Montgomery Place Orchards (Route 9G, Red Hook) — 60+ apple varieties, cider donuts.
days.evening: Gigi (Mediterranean) or Rhinebeck Farmers' Market if Sunday (10am–2pm).

days.title: River Road Long Loop — 52mi / 84km
days.morning: Early start 6:30am. Southwest toward the river, connect to River Road south. Migliorelli Farm Stand (668 River Rd) for mid-ride fuel.
days.afternoon: Loop back north through inland farm roads.
days.evening: Terrapin or Cafe Mutton (757 Columbia St, Hudson — crépinettes, beef tartare, Wed–Fri evenings).

days.title: Hudson Day — Warren Street, Olana, Lil' Deb's
days.morning: Ride or Amtrak one stop to Hudson. Circles Bagels for breakfast.
days.afternoon: Olana State Historic Site (5720 Route 9G) — Frederic Church's 250-acre Persian-inspired estate, grounds free. Warren Street: 50+ antique dealers, Carrie Haddad Gallery.
days.evening: Lil' Deb's Oasis (747 Columbia St, Thu–Sun only, no reservations) — tropical comfort food with Hudson Valley ingredients. James Beard-nominated.

days.title: Shawangunk Ridge — 46mi / 74km, 2,800ft
days.morning: Amtrak south to Poughkeepsie (~35min, ~$12), ride to Walkway Over the Hudson (free, 7am–sunset). Cross to Highland, ride 12 miles south to New Paltz.
days.afternoon: Shawangunk Ridge Road Ride (46 mi paved, 15% pitches) or Mohonk/Minnewaska carriage roads ($10 day-use). Amtrak back north.
days.evening: Recovery dinner in Rhinebeck.

days.title: Farmers' Market Morning → NYC → DTW
days.morning: If Sunday: Rhinebeck Farmers' Market (10am–2pm, 23 E Market St). Amtrak Empire Service south to Penn Station.
days.afternoon: AirTrain and Delta back to DTW.

tips: River Road goes by multiple names — download route from midhudsonbicycleclub.com before leaving; cell signal is spotty.
Lil' Deb's Oasis is Thu–Sun only, no reservations. Go at opening. Plan your Hudson day for Thu–Sun.
GioBatta Alimentari in Tivoli (48 Broadway) is not on most lists. Go for lunch — best food in the valley for its price.
Walkway Over the Hudson is the key to the Shawangunks without a car. Free, 7am–sunset, bikes allowed.
Mid Hudson Bicycle Club (midhudsonbicycleclub.com) has 700+ published routes — single most useful route resource.
Montgomery Place Orchards (Route 9G, Red Hook) is bike-accessible. Apple cider donuts are not optional.

sources.title: Mid Hudson Bicycle Club — Ride with GPS
Best Restaurants in Rhinebeck — The Infatuation

destination.vibe: 80 miles north of Seattle at the foot of the North Cascades, where Chuckanut Drive clings to the cliffs above Samish Bay, a $2 county ferry puts you on a car-optional island 10 miles from downtown, and SR-542 climbs 4,100 feet through old-growth forest to the Coleman Glacier at Artist Point.

flights.notes: DTW→SEA nonstop. July peak $380–470 RT. Getting to Bellingham: Amtrak Cascades from King Street Station to Fairhaven Station (~2h, $27–45, bikes $10 reservation) OR car rental at SEA ($55–80/day) I-5 north 80 miles.

lodging.name: Hotel Leo
lodging.highlights: 1920s boutique hotel with confirmed locked bike room, bike wash station, prohibition-era cocktail bar.

lodging.name: Heliotrope Hotel
lodging.highlights: Renovated 1950s boutique property with dedicated bike washing station, seven distinct room layouts, fire pit yard.

lodging.name: Chrysalis Inn & Spa
lodging.highlights: Waterfront hotel on Bellingham Bay with gas fireplaces, on-site spa, hot tub, Keenan's at the Pier restaurant.

itinerary.overview: Bellingham sits 80 miles north of Seattle on Bellingham Bay, with the North Cascades rising immediately east and Chuckanut Drive — a 23-mile cliff-hugging two-lane above Samish Bay — beginning right at the city's southern edge. Ten days: Bellingham as base (Chuckanut Drive, Lummi Island, rest day) for four days; San Juan Islands for three days of island-hopping; Mt. Baker SR-542 for the penultimate day.

days.title: Arrive — SEA → Bellingham, Fairhaven
days.morning: Fly DTW→SEA. Amtrak Cascades to Fairhaven (2h, bike reservation required) or rent car at SEA.
days.afternoon: Check in, assemble bike. Walk Fairhaven Village. Village Books on 11th St.
days.evening: Aslan Brewing (1330 N Forest St) or Wander Brewing (1807 Dean Ave).

days.title: Chuckanut Drive + Skagit Flats — 50mi / 80km
days.morning: 7:30am south through Fairhaven onto Chuckanut Drive (SR-11) — 23 miles of cliff above Chuckanut Bay. Larrabee State Park (mile 7).
days.afternoon: Bow-Edison: Breadfarm (best bread bakery in northwest Washington). Return north.
days.evening: Wander Brewing. La Fiamma Wood Fire Pizza (200 E Chestnut St).

days.title: Lummi Island via Whatcom Chief Ferry — 47mi
days.morning: Ride 10 miles south on Haxton Way to Gooseberry Point. Whatcom Chief ferry (~$2–4 RT, 6-min crossing).
days.afternoon: 12-mile perimeter road around the island. Return ferry, ride back to Bellingham.
days.evening: Wild Buffalo House of Music or the Shakedown.

days.title: Rest Day — Whatcom Falls + Bellingham
days.morning: Whatcom Falls Park — basalt shelf falls, canyon pool, stone bridge.
days.afternoon: Whatcom Museum Lightcatcher Building (250 Flora St). Kayak at Squalicum Harbor.
days.evening: The Shakedown (1212 N State St).

days.title: Lopez Island — 37mi / 60km
days.morning: Drive to Anacortes. WSF to Lopez (~1h30m, walk-on $15.85).
days.afternoon: Loop Lopez: Shark Reef Sanctuary (seal rookery), Spencer Spit (swim), Fine Mess Bakery in Lopez Village.
days.evening: Overnight: MacKaye Harbor Inn or Odlin County Park camping.

days.title: Orcas Island, Mt. Constitution — 35mi, 1,900ft
days.morning: Inter-island ferry Lopez → Orcas. 9 miles through Crow Valley to Eastsound.
days.afternoon: Mt. Constitution: 5 miles to 2,409-foot summit, CCC stone tower, views to Mt. Baker.
days.evening: Outlook Inn, Eastsound. Dinner at Mijita's (310 A St).

days.title: San Juan Island → Bellingham
days.morning: Ferry Orcas → Friday Harbor. West Side Road to Lime Kiln Point — best land-based orca watching in North America.
days.afternoon: English Camp and American Camp (Pig War history). Ferry to Anacortes, drive north.
days.evening: Back in Bellingham.

days.title: Recovery — Nooksack Valley Farm Loop
days.morning: Flat 25-mile Lynden loop through Whatcom County farm country.
days.afternoon: Kayak at Squalicum Harbor or spa at Chrysalis Inn.
days.evening: Confirm Mt. Baker road status: (360) 856-5700 ext. 515 or nwroadinfo.org.

days.title: Mt. Baker SR-542 — 48mi roundtrip, 4,100ft
days.morning: Drive to Glacier (32 miles, 50 min). Climb SR-542 to Artist Point (5,140ft). Nooksack Falls viewpoint at mile 10.
days.afternoon: Descend 24 miles. Lunch at Graham's Store in Glacier. Drive back.
days.evening: Pack the bike tonight.

days.title: Depart — Bellingham → SEA
days.morning: Tony's Coffee Roastery (2033 Division St). Amtrak Cascades south to King Street Station (2h), Link Light Rail to SeaTac. Or drive rental car.

tips: Amtrak Cascades bike policy: reserve your bike spot ($10 fee) well in advance — July fills fast.
Whatcom Chief ferry (Lummi Island): Whatcom County vessel, NOT WSF. ~$2–4 RT. Runs every 30 min, 6am–10pm.
WSF San Juan Islands: walk-on passengers ($15.85 each way) never need a reservation.
SR-542 / Artist Point road status: typically opens mid-July. Call (360) 856-5700 ext. 515.
July weather: 65–75°F. Mt. Baker can be 20–30°F colder at 5,000ft — pack a rain shell for Day 9.

sources.title: Bicycles on Washington State Ferries — WSDOT

destination.vibe: A Victorian silver-mining town of 6,500 at 7,036 feet in the Arkansas River Valley, circled by more 14,000-foot peaks per square mile than anywhere in North America — where a 22-mile road climb takes you to the Continental Divide at 11,312 feet, the descent is 4,000 vertical feet of fast pavement back to green chile and hot springs, and the entire town fits on two walkable blocks.

flights.notes: DTW→DEN direct. Bag fees trap: bike ~$75–100 each way. Consider BikeFlights.com (~$80–120/way). Drive to Salida: US-285 south from Denver, ~2.5 hours.
DTW→DEN direct. Delta bike fee: $150 each way. Fly into Colorado Springs (COS) as alternative — $180–250 RT, 30 min closer to Salida.

lodging.name: Amigo Motor Lodge
lodging.highlights: 1960s motor lodge renovated to boutique — bike parking on-site, mountain view patio, hot tub. Michelin Select. Roll to Monarch Pass climb in 5 minutes.

lodging.name: Manhattan Hotel Salida
lodging.neighborhood: Downtown Salida — Arkansas River / F Street Arts District
lodging.highlights: Only downtown boutique hotel, above the Boathouse Cantina with direct river views.

itinerary.overview: Salida is the cycling anchor of the Arkansas River Valley at the intersection of three world-class road climbs. Riding is structured around July's weather: brilliant mornings followed by afternoon thunderstorms 1–3pm. Start climbs early, summit before noon.

days.title: Monarch Pass — 46mi / 74km, 4,258ft
days.morning: 7am from Salida (7,036ft). West on US-50 through Poncha Springs. Monarch Pass: 18 miles to 11,312ft, avg 4.3%. Summit lodge has coffee.
days.afternoon: 18-mile descent back. Walk F Street Arts District. Salida Hot Springs Aquatic Center (410 W Rainbow Blvd, ~$10).
days.evening: Amica's Pizzeria or Fritz (European bistro). Evening light on Collegiate Peaks from the river trail.

days.title: Cottonwood Pass — 67mi / 108km, 4,100ft
days.morning: Drive to Buena Vista (35 min north). Cottonwood Pass: 19.5 miles to 12,126ft — third-highest paved road in Colorado. 10% wall at mile 14.
days.afternoon: Descend east back to Buena Vista. Eddyline Brewery on the river for lunch.
days.evening: Salida Hot Springs or Mount Princeton Hot Springs (25 miles north, ~$30). Soulcraft Brewing downtown.

days.title: Poncha Pass + San Luis Valley — 62mi / 100km
days.morning: South on US-285 to Poncha Pass (9,010ft). At summit: the San Luis Valley — 8,000 sq miles of flat high-altitude basin, ringed by mountains.
days.afternoon: Return north over Poncha Pass. Salida Museum (free) or riverside café.
days.evening: Tenderfoot Mountain 'S' hike — 20 min to the best sunset view of Collegiate Peaks. Moonlight Pizza & Brewpub.

tips: Colorado afternoon thunderstorms: start Monarch and Cottonwood by 7am, summit by 10:30am, be descending before noon.
BikeFlights.com (~$80–120/way) beats airline fees ($150–200) for this route.
Altitude: Salida at 7,036ft, Monarch at 11,312ft. Plan one easy day before the big climbs.
Salida Hot Springs (410 W Rainbow Blvd, ~$10/day) is geothermal-fed — open daily.

destination.vibe: A 62,000-person mountain town where Chinese Nationalist soldiers stranded after Mao's 1949 victory planted oolong tea on 1,200-meter ridges and built a Yunnan village that has nothing to do with Thailand — and a 108km road loop climbing 1,700 meters through it waits 40km from your guesthouse, morning clear before the rains arrive.

flights.notes: DTW → ICN → BKK. Add domestic Thai AirAsia or Nok Air BKK(DMK) → CEI, ~$30–45.
DTW → TPE → BKK. Add domestic BKK → CEI separately.

lodging.name: Bed & Bike Hostel (Poshtel)
lodging.highlights: Only cycling-dedicated accommodation in Chiang Rai — bike hire, secure storage, coffee shop on-site.

lodging.name: The Legend Chiang Rai Boutique River Resort & Spa
lodging.highlights: Boutique bungalow resort on the Kok River — spa, pool, riverside dining. Bike rentals on-site.

itinerary.overview: Chiang Rai sits at 580 meters in Thailand's far north, surrounded by mountain ranges pushing to 1,600 meters. July rainy season: reliable morning windows 7am–noon, afternoon thunderstorms daily.

days.title: Doi Mae Salong Loop — 108km, 1,729m
days.morning: 7am north on Route 1 to Mae Chan (34km flat). West on Route 1130 to Santikhiri (Mae Salong) at 1,200m — Yunnan Chinese KMT enclave. Tea and khao soi.
days.afternoon: Descend south via Route 1234. Back by 1pm. Wat Rong Khun (White Temple).
days.evening: Night Bazaar (6–11pm).

days.title: Doi Tung + Golden Triangle — 95km, 1,300m
days.morning: 6:30am north to Doi Tung junction. Climb 18km to 1,389m through Mae Fah Luang Royal Project.
days.afternoon: East via Mekong River Road (Route 1016) to Golden Triangle. Hall of Opium museum (800 THB, 2 hrs).
days.evening: Riverside dinner in Sop Ruak watching longtail boats.

days.title: Chiang Saen River Loop — 80km
days.morning: North on Route 1016. Chiang Saen (55km): Lanna Kingdom former capital, intact walls, Wat Chedi Luang ruins.
days.afternoon: Golden Triangle viewpoint, return south.
days.evening: Saturday Night Walking Street if timing works.

tips: July rain: start all rides by 7am, plan descent or return by 11:30am.
Mae Salong grades: up to 22–24% on steepest ramps. Route 1130 surface well-maintained.
Hall of Opium (Sop Ruak): 2 hours minimum. ~800 THB (~$22).
Hydration: Thai 7-Eleven stocks Pocari Sweat and coconut water everywhere including mountain villages.

sources.title: Doi Mae Salong Chiang Rai road ride — Bicycle Thailand

destination.vibe: Home turf of Tadej Pogačar — where 50 cobbled hairpins climb to 1,611 meters on a road built by Russian WWI prisoners, the Soča runs glacier-turquoise through raw limestone gorges, and summer temperatures in the low 20s mean you can push 80-mile days in the Julian Alps without a 5am alarm.

flights.notes: Connects through Frankfurt (FRA→LJU). Bike fee €100 each way.
Connects through Vienna (VIE→LJU, ~1h). Bike fee €75–100 each way.

lodging.name: Reset Apartments — Bike Resort Kranjska Gora
lodging.highlights: Officially designated 'Bike Resort' — secure indoor bike storage, wash station, drying room. Walking distance to Vršič Pass start.

lodging.name: Hotel Cubo Ljubljana
lodging.neighborhood: Ljubljana Old Town — 400m from Triple Bridge
lodging.highlights: Cycle-friendly certified, confirmed secure bike storage. Perfect first/last night before the Julian Alps.

itinerary.overview: Ljubljana sits at the foot of the Julian Alps, 50km from roads that shaped Tadej Pogačar. Within a day's ride: 1,611 meters on Vršič Pass, 50 hairpin turns, cobbled bends 5–9, the Soča Valley below.

days.title: Ljubljana → Bled via Sava Valley — 75 miles, 1,200m
days.morning: Northwest from Dragon Bridge along Sava River. Stop in Kranj (32km) on a limestone peninsula. Continue to Bled.
days.afternoon: Lake Bled circuit (6km). Ojstrica viewpoint (20% pitch). Optional Pokljuka Plateau biathlon venue.
days.evening: Gostilna Pri Planincu (Grajska cesta 8). Bled Castle night view.

days.title: Vršič Pass Queen Stage — 88km, 1,900m
days.morning: Start Kranjska Gora 7am. Vršič climb (12km to 1,611m): bends 5–9 cobbled at 15–17%, 1916 WWI prisoner-built road. Russian Orthodox Chapel at bend #8.
days.afternoon: Descend 26 hairpins into Trenta Valley. Soča source (cold blue-green pool from limestone grotto). Valley 26km to Bovec.
days.evening: Bovška trout and polenta in Bovec. Wade the Soča (8–12°C ice bath).

days.title: Mangart Saddle — 90km, 2,200m
days.morning: 7am from Bovec. East at Koritnica Valley junction to Mangart. Climb 10km to 2,072m through five dark tunnels (front light required), grades to 25%.
days.afternoon: Descend to Bovec. South to Kobarid (12km) — Hemingway's A Farewell to Arms. Kobarid Museum (European Museum of the Year 1993, one of the best WWI museums in Europe).
days.evening: Return to Kranjska Gora or Ljubljana for flight connection.

tips: Vršič cobbled hairpins (bends 5–9): commit to gear before cobbles — a shift at 15% on rough stone can snap a chain. Wide tires (28–32c) recommended.
Mangart tunnels: front light mandatory, five tunnels, longest ~90 seconds at climbing pace.
Alpine afternoon weather: July thunderstorms by 1–2pm over Triglav. Be off exposed ridges by noon.
Soča River: 8–12°C even in July. A 10-min wade after Day 2 is the best ice-bath at altitude.

sources.title: Cycling Slovenia's Julian Alps — Epic Road Rides
Vršič Pass climb stats — Pjamm Cycling

destination.vibe: The flattest big city in Europe, wrapped in Moorish palaces and orange trees, with 180km of dedicated bike lanes connecting neighborhoods that haven't changed in 500 years — you just have to be on the road before the sun gets serious.

flights.notes: Connects through Istanbul. Bike fees €50–80 each way.
Connects through Madrid (MAD→SVQ, 1h15m shuttle). Bike fees €50–100 each way.

lodging.name: Hotel Corral del Rey
lodging.highlights: 17th-century merchant's townhouse, four courtyards, 13 rooms. Secure bike storage. Most atmospheric address in Seville.

itinerary.overview: Seville has 180km of separated bike lanes through a city of Moorish palaces, orange trees, and flamenco bars. Completely flat. The catch: July temperatures hit 40–43°C by noon. All serious cycling before 9am or after 6:30pm.

destination.vibe: The birthplace of democracy, tragedy, and the gyro — where a 2,500-year-old citadel glows gold at sunset over a city of five million, a souvlaki costs €4, and nothing serious happens before 9pm.

lodging.name: AthensWas Design Hotel
lodging.highlights: 21 rooms on the pedestrian promenade directly below the Acropolis with unobstructed Parthenon views.

itinerary.overview: Athens is not a museum with a city around it — it's a city of five million that happens to have an intact 5th-century BC temple on its central hill.

destination.vibe: The city that taught Europe to eat — 900 years of university life, the world's longest portico, tagliatelle al ragù that has nothing to do with 'spaghetti bolognese,' and terracotta buildings so red they named a political color after them.

The city that went from narco capital to Most Innovative City on Earth in one generation — and is now doing it again with food, coffee, street art, and a metro system that's become a point of civic pride.
No nonstops, no problem — Bangkok rewards the extra hours in transit with 12 million people, temples against skyscrapers, Michelin street food for $1, and the baht weaker than it's been in years.
Twenty-two million people, the world's best street tacos, Diego Rivera murals on government buildings, Frida's blue house, Aztec ruins under a baroque cathedral, mezcal bars in converted mansions.

Seven hills, a 1755 earthquake that flattened everything and got rebuilt fast, 600 years of seafaring bravado, and the most melancholic pop music genre ever invented — all held together by custard tarts and cheap wine.
A city that does everything well and brags about none of it — temples next to bubble tea next to Michelin stalls next to the world's best convenience stores.
Ten million people, 600 years of history, and the world's most obsessive food culture — all running at 5G.
